items.purpose: Creates a tensor from data like a list or array.
Creates a tensor filled with zeros.
Creates a tensor filled with ones.
Creates a tensor with uniform random values in [0, 1).
Creates a tensor with standard normal random values.
Creates a 1-D tensor with values from start to end by step.
Creates a 1-D tensor with evenly spaced steps between start and end.
Creates a tensor that shares memory with a NumPy array.
Returns a tensor with the same data but different shape.
Returns a view of the tensor with a new shape without copying data.
Reorders the dimensions of a tensor according to the given permutation.
Removes dimensions of size one from a tensor.
Inserts a dimension of size one at the specified position.
Concatenates a sequence of tensors along an existing dimension.
Concatenates a sequence of tensors along a new dimension.
Splits a tensor into chunks along a given dimension.
Enables gradient tracking for a tensor in-place.
Computes the gradient of the tensor with respect to graph leaves.
Computes and returns the sum of gradients of outputs with respect to inputs.
Returns a new tensor detached from the computation graph.
Context manager that disables gradient calculation for inference.
Accesses the gradient accumulated for the tensor after backward.
Applies a linear transformation to incoming data.
Applies a 2D convolution over an input signal.
Applies the rectified linear unit activation function element-wise.
Randomly zeroes elements of the input tensor during training.
Normalizes a 2D or 3D input over a batch using running statistics.
A container that chains modules in a sequential order.
Implements the Adam optimization algorithm.
Implements stochastic gradient descent with optional momentum.
Decays the learning rate by gamma every step_size epochs.
Resets gradients of all optimized tensors to zero.
Performs a single optimization step to update parameters.
Implements Adam with decoupled weight decay regularization.
Wraps a dataset to provide batching, shuffling, and parallel loading.
Abstract base class for custom datasets requiring __len__ and __getitem__.
Sets the model in training mode enabling dropout and batch norm updates.
Sets the model in evaluation mode disabling dropout and using batch norm stats.
Saves a serialized object to a file using pickle.
Loads an object saved with torch.save from a file.
Moves a tensor to the specified device such as CPU or CUDA.
Returns True if a CUDA-capable GPU is available.
Releases unused cached GPU memory that can be freed.
Creates a device object representing the specified CUDA device.
Sets the seed for generating random numbers on the current GPU.
Waits for all kernels in all streams on the current device to complete.
